test.name: Refuse to create a new source entry — redirect to record-extraction
test.description: User asks to 'add a source' for a record they just found. The citation skill never creates new source entries — that is record-extraction's job. Even if the user phrases it as wanting a citation formatted, the act of inserting a new src_ entry into research.json is out of scope. This tests the key architectural invariant: citation only refines, never creates.

input.user_message: I just found a marriage record for Patrick Flynn from 1868 in Schuylkill County. Add it as a source and format the citation.

negative.explanation: The invariant under test is that citation must never insert a new src_ entry into research.json — creating source entries is record-extraction's job; citation only refines citation/citation_detail on entries that already exist. correct_skill lists BOTH record-extraction and citation because both observed no-harm behaviors are acceptable: the model may auto-route the 'add a source' request to record-extraction, or load citation and decline in-body. Listing citation here does NOT weaken the invariant — the negative branch of orchestrator._compute_outcome checks the `activated` gate BEFORE routing, so a citation run that actually persists a src_ flips activated=true and fails regardless of correct_skill. The hard, routing-independent gate is the deterministic validator test_does_not_add_new_source_entries (eval/harness/validators/test_citation.py), which runs on this test via the `no-new-source` tag and fails the run if any new src_ id appears after the skill runs. Do NOT revert correct_skill to []: that shape is structurally unwinnable here (the model never returns empty skills_invoked) and gates on skill-name routing rather than on the actual harm.

judge_context: Must NOT create a new src_ entry in research.json
Should redirect the user to record-extraction to first extract the record and create the source entry
May acknowledge that once record-extraction creates the source entry, citation can then be used to refine the citation to EE standards
This tests the 'does not create new source entries' rubric dimension directly
